---
title: "Hosting a GitHub Page"
author: "Rob Wells"
date: "5-29-2026"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
---

# Hosting a GitHub Page - R Cheat Sheet

This exercise will allow you to create a basic web page which will be required for the Investigate Your Community final project option.

For this exercise, you will create a page with your one paragraph story idea. You will then host it on your GitHub page.

## Styled Text

At the core, QMD files are text files, and they allow you to use a very lightweight "markup" language to style text, similar to html, but much less fussy.

Outside of the codeblocks (the gray sections where we write code), we can style any text using standard markdown styling.

This is a nice guide to styling text with markdown: [Markdown guide](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html)

We can create headings similar to html's h1 to h6 with hashtags.

##### This is an h5

We can create links, like this: [google](https://google.com)

We can **bold** or *italic* text.

We can create bulleted lists.

- bullet one
- bullet two
- bullet three

## Code blocks

We can also add special code inside of code blocks -- the gray sections where we write code -- that control how they render in our html document. These output options let us do things like:

- Show or hide the code when we render our html document (echo: true or false)
- Prevent the code from actually running when we render our html document (eval: true or false)
- Hide warnings from outputing when we render our html document (warning: true or false)

There's a [full guide here](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/execution-options.html#output-options).

The code below, for example, will output the text inside the print statement when rendered to html, but the actual code -- the print() function -- will be hidden.

## Create your website

In place of these instructions, you will paste your project one paragraph story idea into this document and any code you've developed so far.

You will need to load libraries and data because rendering a page requires a full R session to execute on the code.

First, create H1 headers

**For example:**

# Income and Vacancies of Baltimore Neighborhoods 2020

## Looking at wealth inequality in Charm City

```{r eval=FALSE, include=TRUE}

# You will write your code in chunks but not execute the data. Use {r eval=FALSE, include=TRUE} in the header so the page will render without running the actual code.
income_vacancy %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = community)) +
  geom_point(aes(y=income_2020), color = "#64D197", stroke=5) +
  geom_point(aes(y=vacant_2020*1000), stroke=5, color = "#FB8AE3") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "2020 Income (Green)", limits=c(0, 62000), labels=scales::dollar_format(),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~./60000, name="2020 Percent Vacancies (Red)", labels = scales::percent))+
  #scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Income and Vacancies of Baltimore Neighborhoods 2020",
    x = "Community",
    caption = "source: BNIA Data - By: Parker Leipzig and Rob Wells"
  )
```

## Set up for hosting

- Create a new folder in your repository called "docs" (if one isn't there already)
- Rename this document as "index.qmd"
- Move "index.qmd" into the "docs" folder

## Render this document as an html page

- Make sure this document is named "index.qmd" and is in the "docs" folder
- Click Render button. It will create an html version of this page, called "index.html"
- Use GitHub Desktop to push your files to your your github repository on the web.
- Access the URL from your Github repository.

## Host html on GitHub

- Go to your github repository on the web, such as https://github.com/djnf-data-2026/casey-mann
- Click on Settings on the top right.
- Scroll down to the Pages section of Settings.
- Change the Branch to /docs and click save.

#### To see your file hosted:

- Back to your GitHub repo on the web: such as https://github.com/djnf-data-2026/casey-mann
- Click on Settings, then Pages
- Under GitHub Pages, you should see "Your site is live at https://djnf-data-2026.github.io/casey-mann/"
- Click that URL
- If it works, email it to robwells\@umd.edu

## REVEL IN YOUR NERD POWERS

# Part 2: Embedding Images

- create a folder in your repository's main branch, call it images

- find any .jpeg, .jpg or .png file and insert it into that folder.

- In a QMD file, display the image as follows:

\#![CAPTION FOR YOUR PHOTO](images/your_link_here.png)

\#![CHATTER FOR YOUR chart](images/xl-refresher-grid_SHEETS.png)

A more advanced technique controlling the width and height

\#![Read the Screen](images/xl-refresher-SHEETS_screen.png){width="100%"}

### Embedding code

From a **Datawrapper Map**

<iframe title="Small Business Reporting, Spring 2024" aria-label="Map" id="datawrapper-chart-z4bny" src="https://datawrapper.dwcdn.net/z4bny/5/" scrolling="no" frameborder="0" style="width: 0; min-width: 100% !important; border: none;" height="500" data-external="1">

</iframe>

```{=html}
<script type="text/javascript">!function(){"use strict";window.addEventListener("message",(function(a){if(void 0!==a.data["datawrapper-height"]){var e=document.querySelectorAll("iframe");for(var t in a.data["datawrapper-height"])for(var r=0;r<e.length;r++)if(e[r].contentWindow===a.source){var i=a.data["datawrapper-height"][t]+"px";e[r].style.height=i}}}))}();
</script>
```

## Embedding video

### Deep Nerd Video

::: {#first_steps}
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/TNP_unReOpw?si=CQC3cG49TShkoJOG" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen>

</iframe>
:::

::: {#second_steps}
### A great lecture on structural racism in journalism

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/DIDHbEZYFSc?si=a-KNzlQgoGjXD6VV" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen>

</iframe>
:::

**Now replace these two videos with something you selected.**

The embed code is in the video share feature

## Instructions to embed a gif

```{r text_to_columns.gif, out.width="100%", echo=FALSE}
### Embed a GIF
#First, download a GIF (extension will be .gif) and load it into "images" folder
#if (knitr::is_html_output()) knitr::include_graphics("images/text_to_columns.gif")  

```

This is a very basic starter but it will get you to the finish line on Saturday. Good luck!

--30--
